美国参议院正在推进对强大加密构成威胁的立法，强加密是数字安全的基石。
虽然支持这项法案的参议员们可能是出于好意，但他们对这项提案的影响却被严重误导了。他们将这项法案命名为消除滥用和猖獗忽视互动技术法案(GAIN IT Act)。加密可确保我们的信息(从敏感的财务和医疗细节到电子邮件和短信)受到保护。但是，“赚取IT法案”将为政府行为者开辟一条广阔的道路，严重破坏强大的加密，使我们的信息处于危险之中。这就是为什么Mozilla加入其他几十个互联网健康和公民社会组织的行列，呼吁美国国会对“赚取IT法案”投反对票。
在遏制在线儿童性虐待材料(CSAM)的幌子下，该法案威胁到在线安全以及为用户生成内容提供便利的平台。它要求在线平台做出选择，要么做出选择，要么放弃让互联网成为创作者充满活力的环境的法律保护。“最佳做法”将由司法部长威廉·巴尔(William Barr)领导的一个未经选举产生的委员会决定，似乎有可能阻碍强大的加密。人权、言论自由、数字隐私和安全的倡导者都同意，这项立法是解决真正问题的严重误导的尝试。我们需要确保国会认识到“赚取IT法案”是错误的解决方案，并投票反对该法案。除了对加密的偷袭外，“赚取IT法案”还威胁到第230条，这项法律保护平台对其用户发布的内容承担特定类型的责任。自1996年以来，第230条通过遏制法律行动的威胁，鼓励了互联网的增长。但“赚取IT法案”将创建一个未经选举产生的委员会，由执法部门主导，由司法部长巴尔领导，为在线平台和服务编写定义不清的“最佳实践”，并将其迅速纳入法律。虽然对现行法律责任制度的改革可能值得考虑，但将空白支票交给律政司并不是进行这些改革的正确方法。“赚取IT法案”的支持者拒绝在提案文本中增加对加密的保护，也拒绝将其置于委员会“最佳实践”的范围之外，明确表示该法案实际上只是一种偷偷摸摸的方式，目的是进行一场被误导的反对加密的战争。更重要的是，该委员会将由司法部长巴尔领导，巴尔有进行非法大规模监控的历史，他已经明确表示，他希望获得一个政府密钥来破解加密，这让他有更多的筹码向平台施压，要求破解加密。
我们都同意剥削儿童是可怕的，但“赚取IT法案”是一个危险的提议，对开放的互联网弊大于利。在提交给国会的请愿书上签名，反对这种隐蔽的加密攻击。
note_en:
The U.S. Senate is advancing legislation that creates a threat to strong encryption, the bedrock of digital security.
 While the senators championing the bill, which they’ve named the Eliminating Abusive and Rampant Neglect of Interactive Technologies Act (EARN IT Act), may have good intentions, they are seriously misguided about the impact of their proposal.   Encryption ensures our information, from our sensitive financial and medical details to emails and text messages, is protected. But the EARN IT Act will create a broad path for government actors to seriously undermine strong encryption, putting our information at risk.  That’s why Mozilla is joining dozens of other internet health and civil society organizations in calling on the U.S. Congress to vote no on the EARN IT Act.
    Under the guise of curbing online child sexual abuse material (CSAM), the bill threatens online security as well as the platforms that facilitate user generated content. It asks online platforms to make the choice between potentially onerous “best practices” – which will be determined by an unelected commission headed by Attorney General William Barr and seem likely to obstruct strong encryption – or giving up the legal protections that have made the internet a vibrant environment for creators.   Advocates for human rights, free speech and digital privacy and security all agree that this legislation is a seriously misguided attempt to address a real problem. We need to make sure that Congress sees that the EARN IT Act is the wrong solution and votes against the bill.   In addition to a sneak attack on encryption, the EARN IT Act also threatens Section 230, the law that protects platforms from certain kinds of liability for content posted by their users.  Since 1996, Section 230 has encouraged internet growth by keeping the threat of legal action at bay. But the EARN IT Act would create an unelected commission, dominated by law enforcement and led by Attorney General Barr, to write ill-defined “best practices” for online platforms and services that would be fast-tracked into law. While reforms to the current liability regime may be worth considering, handing a blank check to the Attorney General is not the right approach to those reforms.   Supporters of the EARN IT Act have refused to add protection for encryption in the text of the proposal, or to place it outside the scope of the commission’s “best practices,” making it clear that the bill is actually just a sneaky way to pursue a misguided war against encryption. What’s more, the commission would be led by Attorney General Barr, who has a history of conducting illegal mass surveillance and who has made it clear that he wants a government key to break encryption, giving him even more leverage to pressure platforms to break encryption.
 We all agree that child exploitation is horrific, but the EARN IT Act is a risky proposition that would do more harm than good to the open internet.   Sign the petition to Congress opposing this veiled attack on encryption.
title: 反对“赚取IT”法案

note:
我也很幸运早早地阅读了@Coding_Career，我完全被打动了！这是我刚开始从事开发工作时希望拥有的那本书。
我希望我换工作的时候有这本书。它帮助你了解你应该利用哪些技能，并真正鼓励你不要低估自己！
指向高价值博客文章的链接已经让这本书物有所值，更不用说肖恩提供的令人惊叹的洞察力和背景了。必须购买#CodeNewbies希望打入或高级工程师寻求增长！
哇!。在“编程职业手册”中分享的知识和经验的巨大财富怎么夸张都不为过。我喜欢肖恩能够将自己的经历和他在他人职业生涯中观察到的东西概括为有助于他人提升自己职业生涯的原则。五星级⭐。
对于那些寻求程序员职业建议，但厌倦了被告知必须做X或永远不做Y的人，肖恩分享了他在这些有时势不可挡的水域中航行的个人经验和知识，希望你能找到一些有用的东西。这就像你口袋里有一位体贴的大哥！(如果你在手机上阅读)。
我喜欢他提供的关于职业倦怠的信息，因为这是新开发人员在职业生涯早期遇到的最大障碍之一。Shawn包含了许多来自其他内容创建者的精彩资源和图表的链接。关于谈判的部分是我在职业生涯早期就可以用到的。
肖恩谈到了经验丰富的工程师和管理人员通过艰苦的方式学到的东西，或者通过已经制作出来的东西--但方式是所有经验层次的人都能接触到。直到现在才有人谈论的事情！
我正在通读swyx的@Coding_职业生涯，我真的印象深刻。这本书很厚，但读起来很清淡。对于进入这个行业的人来说，有很多有用的背景信息。
这本书可以取名为“Hacker News：The Good Parts”(这本书的书名可能是最好的，我是真心的)&“黑客新闻”，所有基于经验的职业成功建议，没有一句有毒的废话。
我该怎么办？告诉我该怎么做！关于如何成为一名程序员，没有一个正确的答案，更不用说一个快乐而成功的程序员了。然而，有数以千计的故事是关于人们如何自己破解编码职业的！肖恩已经把他的故事组合在一起，并提供了大量实用的现实世界的建议！忠告最棒的地方是什么？你不必吃药。编程职业手册是一本精彩的集合，它收集了哪些工作有效，哪些工作可能不适合您！无论你是编程新手还是像我一样的老程序员，这些指导方针和策略都可以融入到你的编程生活和职业生涯中--学习如何成为一名伟大的程序员永远不会太晚！
note_en:
I was also lucky enough to read @Coding_Career early and I was thoroughly blown away! It’s the book I wish I had when I started my dev career.
 I wish I had this book when I was switching jobs. It helps you understand what skills you should leverage and really encourages you not underlevel yourself!
 The links to high-value blog posts already make the book worth it, never mind the amazing insight and context provided by Shawn. Must buy for #CodeNewbies looking to break in or senior engineers looking to grow!
 Wow! The immense wealth of knowledge and experience shared in "The Coding Career Handbook" cannot be overstated. I love how Shawn is able to take his own experience and what he's observed in the careers of others and generalize it to principles that will help others level up their own careers. 5 stars ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
 For anyone looking for career advice as a coder but is tired of being told they have to do X or never do Y. Shawn is sharing his personal experiences and knowledge of navigating these sometimes overwhelming waters in the hopes you might find something useful. It's like having a caring big brother in your pocket! (if you read on your phone)
 I love that he included information about burnout as this is one of the biggest hurdles new developer encounter early in their career. Shawn has included links to many wonderful resources and graphs from other content creators. The section on negotiation is one I could have used early on in my career.
 Shawn talks about things that experienced engineers and managers learn "the hard way" or by having "already made it" -- but in a way that is accessible for people at all levels of experience. Things that NO ONE talks about, until now!
 I’m reading through swyx’s @Coding_Career and I’m genuinely impressed. It’s very dense but reads very lightly. Lots of useful context for someone coming into the industry.
 The book could've been called (and I mean this in the best possible way, I really do) "Hacker News: The Good Parts." All the experience-based advice for career success, none of the toxic nonsense.
 What should I do? Just tell me what to do! There's no One Right Answer for how to become a coder, much less a happy and "successful" one. What there is, however, are thousands of stories of how folks have cracked the coding career themselves! Shawn has put together his story, with a ton of practical and pragmatic real world advice! The great thing about advice? You don't have to take it. The Coding Career Handbook is a wonderful collection of what's worked and what may not work for you! These are guidelines and tactics that you can incorporate into your coding life and career whether you're a code newbie or a code oldie like me - it's never too late to learn how to be a great programmer!
title: 编码职业手册
